message:
fix(sync): pool return-waker notification, contended_mutex poison discrimination, mutex/rwlock test coverage

Pool (major): Add return_wakers notification mechanism so that
PooledResource::return_inner and discard_inner immediately wake waiting
acquirers instead of relying on the next process_returns call. Without
this, a resource returned while no other code polls the pool could leave
waiters stuck until timeout. Implementation: PooledResource carries an
optional Arc<Mutex<Vec<Waker>>> populated by GenericPool; return/discard
drains and wakes all registered wakers. WaitForNotification::poll now
calls process_returns on entry (so idle/active counts reflect recently
returned resources) and registers in the return_wakers list.

Pool (warmup): Refactor warmup() to use CreateSlotReservation, ensuring
concurrent acquire() calls see accurate capacity during warmup. Previously
warmup could overshoot max_size when racing with concurrent acquires.
New commit_create_slot_as_idle() puts warmup resources directly into the
idle queue without incrementing active or total_acquisitions. New
committed_manually() disarms the reservation drop guard when the caller
handles accounting.

ContendedMutex: Fix contention detection to distinguish WouldBlock
(genuine contention) from Poisoned (not contention — the lock is broken).
Previously, a poisoned mutex was counted as contention because try_lock()
returning Err was treated uniformly. Add test verifying poisoned lock
does not increment the contentions counter.

Mutex: Add tests for OwnedMutexGuard (try_lock read/write/relock cycle,
async lock path) and Mutex::default.

RwLock: Formatting cleanup on test assertion macros from the previous
commit (rustfmt line-length compliance).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
